---
title: "Data Cleaning of Floods in New York State from 2018-2022"
date: "2026-02-25"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(lubridate)
library(tidyr)
```


```{r}

# list all csv and gz files in my dataset
files <- list.files(
  path = "datasets/",
  pattern = "\\.csv$|\\.csv\\.gz$",  
  full.names = TRUE
)

# check on file names and types
file_info <- tibble(
  path = files,
  type = case_when(
    str_detect(files, "locat") ~ "locations",
    str_detect(files, "det") ~ "details",
    str_detect(files, "fat") ~ "fatalities",
    TRUE ~ "other"
  ),
  year = str_extract(files, "\\d{4}")
)

# create function to read all files
read_file <- function(file) {
  read_csv(file, show_col_types = FALSE)
}

# read all files by type before merging
locations_all <- file_info |> 
  filter(type == "locations") |> 
  pull(path) |> 
  lapply(read_file) |> 
  bind_rows()

details_all <- file_info |> 
  filter(type == "details") |> 
  pull(path) |> 
  lapply(read_file) |> 
  bind_rows()

fatalities_all <- file_info |> 
  filter(type == "fatalities") |> 
  pull(path) |> 
  lapply(read_file) |> 
  bind_rows()

# use event_id to combine all events
all_storms <- locations_all |> 
  left_join(details_all, by = "EVENT_ID") |> 
  left_join(fatalities_all, by = "EVENT_ID") |> 
  janitor::clean_names() |> 
  filter(
    state == "NEW YORK",
    event_type %in% c("Flood", "Heavy Rain", "Flash Flood")
  ) |> 
  janitor::clean_names()

# check on storms
dim(all_storms)
table(all_storms$event_type)
```


Creating function to turn damage into numeric var type
```{r}
convert_damage <- function(damage_str) {
  if (is.na(damage_str) || damage_str == "") return(0)
  
  # extract the numeric part
  num <- as.numeric(str_extract(damage_str, "[0-9.]+"))
  
  # multiply based on suffix
  if (str_detect(damage_str, "K")) {
    num <- num * 1e3
  } else if (str_detect(damage_str, "M")) {
    num <- num * 1e6
  } else if (str_detect(damage_str, "B")) {
    num <- num * 1e9
  }
  
  return(num)
}

# apply the function to all_storms
all_storms <- all_storms |> 
  mutate(
    damage_property = sapply(damage_property, convert_damage),
    damage_crops    = sapply(damage_crops, convert_damage),
    total_damage        = damage_property + damage_crops
  )
```


Make each row equal to a day to do time-series modeling.
```{r}
all_storms <- all_storms |>
  mutate(
    begin_dt = dmy_hms(begin_date_time),  # "12-JAN-18 20:00:00"
    end_dt   = dmy_hms(end_date_time)
  )
```

```{r}
# expand each event to all days it spans
ny_floods_daily <- all_storms |> 
  rowwise() |> 
  mutate(days = list(seq.Date(as.Date(begin_dt), as.Date(end_dt), by = "day"))) |> 
  unnest(days) |> 
  ungroup()

# aggregate per day
daily_agg <- ny_floods_daily |> 
  group_by(days) |> 
  summarize(
    total_damage = sum(damage_property + damage_crops, na.rm = TRUE),
    flood_flag = 1,  # 1 = flood happened
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# aggregate per day (injuries and death)

```
